title: End-to-End Booking Flow
summary: Search, hold, pay, confirm — orchestrated with sagas and clear compensation if payment fails.
keyTakeaways: API gateway routes search to read services, booking to write service.
Hold service writes to inventory DB; payment service calls Stripe.
On payment success: confirm hold; on failure: release hold (compensating action).
Kafka events update search index and send confirmation emails async.
Read replicas serve 'my trips'; primary handles commits.
Admin overrides need audit log separate from user-facing booking row.

body.source:
flowchart TB Guest["Guest app"] Inv[("Inventory DB
PostgreSQL")] ES[("Elasticsearch
listings index")] Redis[("Redis
hot listing cache")] subgraph readPath ["Read path — search and browse"] direction LR SearchAPI["Search API"] Guest -->|"① browse / filter"| SearchAPI SearchAPI -->|"② query index"| ES SearchAPI -.->|"③ cache hit"| Redis Guest -->|"④ GET listing detail"| SearchAPI end subgraph writePath ["Write path — hold, pay, confirm"] direction LR BookAPI["Booking API"] Stripe["Stripe"] Kafka[[Kafka]] Guest -->|"⑤ POST /hold"| BookAPI BookAPI -->|"⑥ lock slot"| Inv Guest -->|"⑦ POST /confirm"| BookAPI BookAPI -->|"⑧ charge card"| Stripe BookAPI -->|"⑨ confirm → COMMIT"| Inv BookAPI -->|"⑩ booking.confirmed"| Kafka Kafka -.->|"⑪ async index update"| ES end
body.caption: Reservations split fuzzy search (Elasticsearch) from exact booking (PostgreSQL + Stripe). Kafka updates the index after commit.

items: ① Browse / filter — Guest runs date, price, and location filters against the Search API.
② Query index — Search API queries Elasticsearch inverted index — never scans inventory rows.
③ Cache hit — Hot listing cards served from Redis when the same queries repeat.
④ GET listing detail — Guest opens a listing page; photos and description from search index or cache.
⑤ POST /hold — Guest reserves a slot; Booking API starts a short transaction.
⑥ Lock slot — Inventory row locked in PostgreSQL (SELECT FOR UPDATE or serializable isolation).
⑦ POST /confirm — Guest submits payment with Idempotency-Key to prevent double charge on retry.
⑧ Charge card — Booking API calls Stripe; payment success required before confirm.
⑨ Confirm → COMMIT — Hold converted to sale in one atomic transaction.
⑩ booking.confirmed — Event published to Kafka after commit (outbox pattern).
⑪ Async index update — Consumer updates Elasticsearch availability — derived data, not dual-write.

body.source:
sequenceDiagram
    participant G as Guest
    participant API as Booking API
    participant Inv as Inventory DB
    participant St as Stripe
    participant K as Kafka
    G->>API: POST /holds
    API->>Inv: BEGIN txn, lock slot
    Inv-->>API: holdId
    API-->>G: hold expires 15m
    G->>API: POST /confirm + Idempotency-Key
    API->>St: charge
    St-->>API: paymentId
    API->>Inv: confirm hold and COMMIT
    API->>K: booking.confirmed
    API-->>G: 201 confirmed
body.caption: Happy-path sequence from hold through payment to confirmed booking.
Booking saga with compensation

body.title: In practice
body.text: Expedia and Booking.com pipelines mirror this: Elasticsearch for discovery, PostgreSQL for reservations, Stripe/Adyen for payments, Temporal or custom sagas for multi-step checkout.
